hooks.command: REPO=$(git remote -v 2>/dev/null | head -1 | awk '{print $2}' | sed 's|.*[:/]||; s|\.git$||'); BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); DIR=$(pwd); if [ -n "$REPO" ]; then MSG="Current repo: $REPO | branch: ${BRANCH:-detached} | dir: $DIR"; else MSG="Not in a git repo. Working dir: $DIR"; fi; jq -nc --arg ctx "$MSG" '{hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:"SessionStart",additionalContext:$ctx}}'

INPUT=$(cat); SESSION=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // "default"'); DIR="$HOME/.claude/state"; mkdir -p "$DIR"; find "$DIR" -name 'turn-counter-*' -mtime +1 -delete 2>/dev/null; F="$DIR/turn-counter-${SESSION}"; N=$(cat "$F" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); N=$((N + 1)); echo "$N" > "$F"; if [ "$N" -eq 1 ]; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"UserPromptSubmit","additionalContext":"User-configured INNOVATION FAN-OUT hook (fires on the first prompt of the session = the problem statement). Unless this task is trivial (a quick question, a lookup, or a one-line fix), kick off a parallel fan-out BEFORE implementing, to make up for sparse global context and to innovate many times. In ONE message, spawn parallel agents with the Agent tool using model haiku: (a) 3-4 agents each doing a very deep, DIVERGENT brainstorm of the problem statement, each from a different framing (first-principles, constraints and edge-cases, alternative architectures, user/client value); (b) 2-3 agents doing an ADVERSARIAL review of the relevant existing code (fragile assumptions, hidden coupling, what a harsh critic would attack). Run them concurrently. Then synthesize TIGHTLY: gather the distinct innovations and risks, pick the strongest, and fold them into your approach before coding. Do not dump raw agent output; give a short synthesis. If the task is trivial, skip this silently."}}'; fi

jq -r '.tool_input.command' | grep -qE 'gh pr (create|edit)' && echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"A PR was just created or edited. Confirm the PR is OPEN (gh pr view --json state -q .state), then run the full PR pipeline IN ORDER: (1) run the /pr-review-toolkit:review-pr skill, (2) run the /simplify skill, (3) run the /client-value-maximizer skill, and (4) update the changelog: add a CHANGELOG.md entry for any user-facing or behavioral change (house style: simple English, business terms, ASCII flow for any process), and update README.md only if it documents the changed surface. Do NOT edit CLAUDE.md (self-modification guarded). Skip missing files; do not create new docs files unless asked."}}' || true

echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreCompact","additionalContext":"CONTEXT PRESERVATION BEFORE COMPACTION: Re-read CLAUDE.md and frontend/CLAUDE.md after compaction. Check git status for uncommitted work. Review .claude/rules/ for project conventions. Any plan in progress should be re-checked against the plan file in docs/plans/. Do NOT lose track of what branch you are on or what task the user asked for."}}'
printf '\a' > /dev/tty 2>/dev/null; osascript -e 'display notification "Claude needs your input" with title "Claude Code" sound name "Glass"'
